# Error Handling Checklist: Reviewer-ready PR packet contract

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-012 requirements define exact, recoverable, non-destructive behavior for invalid PR packets before task generation.
**Created**: 2026-06-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Failure Mode Coverage

- [x] CHK001 Are stale placeholders, banned labels, internal title tokens, missing headings, missing source markers, missing verification evidence, and missing scope evidence defined as validator rejection cases? [Completeness, Spec FR-007 to FR-013]
- [x] CHK002 Is missing or unreadable packet input behavior specified with a deterministic exit code, stderr shape, remediation evidence, and zero PR creation attempts? [Resolved, Spec FR-015A/FR-015B, Data Model PR Packet, Quickstart Scenario 7]
- [x] CHK003 Is malformed packet behavior, including invalid JSON or schema-invalid packet shape, distinguished from rendered-content validation failures? [Resolved, Spec FR-015A/FR-015B, Data Model Packet Validation Result, Contract]
- [x] CHK004 Are stale or placeholder-filled rendered packet bodies covered outside code fences, comments, generated fixtures, `.process`, and generated zones? [Coverage, Spec FR-007/FR-008A/FR-016B]
- [x] CHK005 Are safe-refinement failures outside sanctioned prose markers specified as blocking validation failures before PR creation? [Completeness, Spec FR-016A/FR-016B]

## Validator Diagnostics

- [x] CHK006 Are validation failure exit codes specified separately from usage or malformed-input errors? [Clarity, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK007 Are deterministic stderr requirements specified for validation failures and input errors so fixture tests can compare operator-facing diagnostics? [Resolved, Spec FR-015C, Data Model Packet Validation Result, Plan Phase 0]
- [x] CHK008 Does validation failure evidence name the failed rule, packet target, affected section or field, and excerpt or hash evidence when available? [Completeness, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK009 Are validation JSON fields defined for status, packet identity, mode, PR target, title/body paths or values, rule outcomes, failures, remediation evidence, `pr_blocked`, and timestamps? [Completeness, Spec FR-014, Contract]

## Stop Ordering And Workflow Evidence

- [x] CHK010 Is PR creation explicitly blocked before `gh pr create` for invalid packets? [Completeness, Spec FR-005/FR-013, Plan Summary]
- [x] CHK011 Is workflow evidence required when validation blocks a packet before stopping? [Completeness, Spec FR-015, Data Model Workflow Event]
- [x] CHK012 Is the validation result path deterministic and packet-specific under the feature `.process/pr-packets/<packet_id>/validation.json` tree? [Clarity, Spec FR-014, Data Model PR Packet]

## Resume And Split-PR Recovery

- [x] CHK013 Is resume behavior after fixing an invalid packet specified so the corrected packet is revalidated and stale failed validation JSON cannot continue blocking? [Resolved, Spec FR-015D, Data Model Packet Resume Evidence, Quickstart Scenario 8]
- [x] CHK014 Does split-PR failure behavior preserve evidence for earlier successful split PRs and define where resume continues after the invalid packet is fixed? [Resolved, Spec FR-015E, Data Model Packet Resume Evidence, Workflow Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK015 Can one failed split packet be identified without hiding other packet outcomes? [Coverage, Data Model Packet Validation Result]
- [x] CHK016 Is broad post-create auto-repair explicitly out of scope so recovery requirements stay bounded to pre-create validation and resume? [Consistency, Spec FR-018]

## Notes

- Initial error-handling checklist pass produced 5 true gaps for remediation before task generation.
- Remediation pass 1 completed on 2026-06-12 after updating `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, `quickstart.md`, `contracts/pr-packet.schema.json`, and `docs/ai/specs/.process/PRSG-012-workflow.md`; all five gaps are resolved.
